---
title: "About"
---

*All models are wrong, but some are useful.* - George E. P. Box

I've always found statistics hard to remember, yet so crucial for so many 
scientific questions. Knowing when to pick a test/technique allows for insights
we all strive for, otherwise you are creating "useless" models. 

My goal is to create a knowledgebase of stats, answering the following:  
-  "What question is this test/technique answering" and   
-  "Am I allowed to use it for my instance?" 

The first question tackles the point and interpretation of a test/technique. The
second talks about the assumptions they make about the data (and more). You can 
have a test that does exactly what you need, but it makes assumptions about 
your data for it to be used that just don't hold in your instance, making it a
"useless" model.
